name: CPT
oneliner: Additional pre-training on domain-specific data (code, medical, legal) to inject specialized knowledge — same loss as pre-training, 2-10% of the cost.
explanation: Continued pre-training takes an existing general-purpose base model and runs additional pre-training on a domain-specific corpus using the same next-token prediction objective. The key challenge is catastrophic forgetting, which is mitigated by mixing in 10 to 30 percent general data and using a lower learning rate. Notable examples include Code Llama trained on 500 billion code tokens, Meditron for medical text, SaulLM for legal documents, and Minerva for math and science. CPT typically costs 2 to 10 percent of the original pre-training budget.
fundamentals: Loss: identical to pre-training (autoregressive CE). Only data distribution changes. Hyperparams: lr 1e-5 to 5e-5, cosine or constant schedule. Data mixing: ~70-90% domain + 10-30% general replay.

foundational_papers.title: Don't Stop Pretraining: Adapt Pretrained Language Models to Diverse Text Domains
Code Llama: Open Foundation Models for Code
Minerva: Solving Quantitative Reasoning Problems with Language Models
